prompt:
Every Monday our release-audit job compares the manifest we rolled to staging against what's actually running in prod and posts the drift to the channel. The comparison is reflect.DeepEqual plus a homegrown struct dump, and it cries wolf constantly: DeployedAt never matches (obviously), the CD controller reorders port lists, a service with no env vars comes back as an empty map where we recorded nil, and CPU requests round-trip through the scheduler with float jitter in the 4th decimal. Meanwhile the drift we actually care about — image tags, replica counts, env values — is buried in the noise.

I want this rebuilt on google/go-cmp instead of our hand-rolled mess. We pin dependencies on this team: add github.com/google/go-cmp to go.mod at exactly v0.7.0 and commit the matching go.sum entries (the module isn't in the manifest yet — this box can reach the module proxy, so fetch it properly rather than hand-editing). Then write the package itself, package reldrift at the repo root (drift.go, or split files however you like):

- type Service struct — Name, Image string; Replicas int; CPU float64; Ports []int; Env map[string]string; DeployedAt time.Time
- SameService(a, b Service) bool — equivalence under our house rules: DeployedAt is ignored entirely; CPU compares with absolute tolerance 0.001; Ports are order-insensitive; nil and empty count as equal for the map and the slices
- DiffService(a, b Service) string — "" when equivalent under the same rules, otherwise a readable diff that names the drifted fields (this is what lands in the channel)
- BuildReport(before, after []Service) (Report, error) — Report has Added, Removed, Changed []string and Details map[string]string. Services are keyed by Name: Added = names only in after, Removed = names only in before, Changed = present in both but not equivalent; all three sorted ascending. Details carries the DiffService text for changed services only. A name appearing twice inside either slice is an error, message exactly: duplicate service "NAME"

drift_test.go is the acceptance suite and it uses go-cmp itself, so the dependency genuinely has to be wired up before anything compiles. Don't touch the test file. Done = go test ./... green.
